package: Goal 12: Coupling-Activated Traversable ER=EPR Channel Dynamics

scope.family: encoded Bell resources with product five-qubit right-mouth blocks
scope.dynamics: bounded encoded Clifford Bell-coupling activation U_couple(alpha)

core_claim: Low-order physical entropy/min-cut shadows through the code distance do not determine coupling-activated traversable transfer; transfer is controlled by the relation between the resource pairing and the coupling activation map.

theorem_style_claims.claim: For resource pairing pi and coupling activation alpha, activated channel capacity equals |{i: alpha(i)=pi(i)}|.
For product distance-d right-mouth stabilizer encodings, the resource pairing pi is invisible to labeled physical entropy diagnostics through order d.
Wrong activations have zero transfer on the representative twisted/aligned controls even though the entropy shadows match.

bounded_exhaustive_evidence.scope: aligned and twisted two-mouth resources with identity and twisted activation couplings
all resource pairings and all activation maps for m<=3

proof_obligations.claim: Emit the coupling-activated ER=EPR channel dynamics certificate.
Regression check for the Goal 12 certificate.

diagnostic_hierarchy.low_order_physical_entropy_through_dista: insufficient for the encoded mouth map
diagnostic_hierarchy.algebraic_connectivity_plus_activation: determines transfer capacity in the declared finite family
diagnostic_hierarchy.otoc_like_support_growth: points to the algebraic receiver and distinguishes wrong activations

known_vs_new.known_derived: Standard stabilizer distance, encoded Bell transfer, and Clifford Pauli-transfer bookkeeping.
known_vs_new.new_programmatic_contribution: Packages a finite coupling-activated ER=EPR benchmark where entropy-matched encoded resources require the right algebraic activation to become traversable.

limitations: This is an exact finite stabilizer/Clifford coupling benchmark, not a continuum-gravity theorem, not a chaotic many-body traversable-wormhole simulation, and not an approximate-QEC theorem. Full encoded-block diagnostics reveal the mouth map.
next_frontier: Replace the encoded Bell-transfer instrument with richer tensor-network or many-body dynamics and test whether algebraic bridge connectivity still predicts transfer better than entropy shadows.
